titleStmt.title: SII 11.15: original edition by C. R. Krishnamacharlu, R. S. Panchamukhi, N. Lakshminaraya Rao and H. K. Narasimhaswami
No. 15. (B.K. No. 105 of 1926-27). CHIÑCHLI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT. ON THREE FACES OF A BROKEN PILLAR LYING NEAR A RUINED TEMPLE.

summary.p: The mutilated record refers itself to the reign of Amōghavarsha I and bears the � Śaka 793 (mistake for 795), Vijaya. It registers the gift of a gōsāsa, a tank and a garden by a private individual (name lost) after washing the feet of the Fifty-six Mahājanas of Chiṁchila, when Dēvaṇṇayya was governing Beḷvola Three Hundred. The inscription was written by Paramēśvara-Bhaṭṭa and engraved by Indagōṭi.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svastyamōghavarṣa śrī pṛthuvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja para tītasaṁvatsara śataṅga
ḷeḻnūṟatombhattamūṟaneya vijayasaṁvatsaraṁ pravarttisa tadantarggata ṟeymaṁ ciñciladayya
ttaṟuvaruṁ mahājanada kālaṁ kaḻcikoṭṭu gōsāsa(ṁ) miḻdōṁ sōdaka pūrvaka ṟeyamma gōsāsa mi
ḻdō keṟeĀramesahita dēvaṇṇayyaṁ beḷvolamūnūṟunāḍāḷe Inda ḷāḻugeyyuttire Intīdha
rmamaṁ geydōṁ nīruṇisuṁkaṁ biṭṭi modalāgi Ellamaṁ biṭṭōṁ yasya vā yō harēti vasundharāṁ
ṣaṣṭiṁ varṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭhāyāṁ jāyatē krimiḥ ©
paramēśvarabhaṭṭara likhitaṁ candrārkka dharārṇnavaṁ nilke
Indagōṭeya besaṁ Ī dharmmamaṁ kādōṁ svarggi yakuṁ ©
Digital edition of SII 11.15 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
